title: Samatha-Vipassana.To play the media you will need to either update your browser to a recent version or update yourFlash plugin
justification: This talk provides the most comprehensive and coherent exploration of Samatha and Vipassana, tracing the Buddha's personal journey through both practices, explaining their distinct purposes, and culminating in the teaching of the Satipatthana Sutta to lay practitioners. It offers rich historical context, personal anecdotes about Thai forest masters, clear technical explanations of jhanas and the seven factors of enlightenment, and addresses the practical distinction between the 'one-way path' (Ekayana) and the 'two-way path' (Dvayana). The narrative arc from the Buddha's disillusionment through his awakening provides excellent literary structure.
summary: This talk traces the Buddha's spiritual journey from sensual indulgence through absorption meditations and asceticism to his discovery of Vipassana, explaining how Samatha develops concentrated states while Vipassana purifies the heart through direct investigation. It culminates in discussing the Satipatthana Sutta as the 'one-way path' accessible to lay practitioners, distinguishing it from the combined Samatha-Vipassana approach.

relevance.note: Primary focus on samatha and vipassanā meditation approaches
